mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving positive earnings revisions and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals supply chain normalization and capacity expansion, reducing input cost inflation and supporting near-term equity risk appetite over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with S&P 500 declines in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating headline inflation triggers market expectations for Federal Reserve tightening, raising discount rates and pressuring equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: High inflation prints are followed by positive S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly GDP growth exceeding potential output signals broad-based earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth exceeding 2% annually correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising non-farm payrolls combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market, sustaining consumer spending and equity valuations over a month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeding 100k with a rising unemployment rate fails to predict positive S&P 500 returns in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1 million units alongside industrial production above 105 indicate broad-based economic acceleration, providing a positive near-term catalyst for equity markets.
failure_condition: High housing starts with rising equity volatility do not produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations.
